All right, the Wolfs in the House fear daily motivation slash skills training. And listen, here's the deal. We are in the thick of it here with certification training right now. Wow. Up until now, I've been explaining how the system works. Here are the tools. Here's the things that essentially in your toolbox you're going to use to create logical certainty, emotional certainty, right? Well, we're prospecting that we formally cross over now into exactly what you're going to say in the order in which you're going to say it, why it has to be this way. And I've got to tell you guys, this is the spot in the sale. When you're asking questions, it's the spot that you can really show that you care, that you're not like other salespeople, that you actually care about the prospect, that you want the best for them, that you're going to take the time to customize whatever you have to fit their needs. I got to tell you, the whole, the whole show, tell me this pen business, right? It's all based on this idea that how could you go about trying to sell anything to anyone unless you knew certain facts about them? You couldn't do it. You can't, you, you cannot effectively sell someone something unless you know what their needs are, what their values are, what their beliefs are, what their pain points are. If they can afford it or else you waste a lot of time. Now I'm going to talk about this in some of the other more specific topics during the week, you know, the weekly motive, you know, during the week, this motivation, that's for motivations. But for right now, I want you to understand that, like you're at this magic moment in your own life right now where you've, you know, you've paid good money for this, right? You made an investment in yourself to learn these skills at the highest level. This is the point now where you'll see, if you haven't really started going through this module yet, the exercises are crucial now because you're at the point right now where you're going to start using the things that you create in your exercises, those language patterns to construct your straight line scripts. It's all building blocks to get to this point, literally where you're going to have a turnkey solution to go from open to close and sound awesome the entire time. You know, we talk about these 10 rules of straight line prospecting. That's what it's really all about. It's this idea that no matter how powerful, no matter how direct that your marketing campaign is, and again, you have some really great marketing campaigns out there, it's not only going to bring in qualified buyers. It's just not. You're going to have the tire kickers, those looky-loos, right? Same name, different thing, different name, same thing, right? And you can have people that just really don't even want to be in your private, I kind of got dragged there. And this is the spot where the average salesperson gets destroyed. They spend an inordinate amount of time talking to people they simply should not be talking to. You can't turn non-buyers into buyers. Bottom line. I'll tell you a very funny story. When I, my first business was a paper route. I was eight years old knocking on doors and I thought that I would grow my route effectively by making a list of all the doors in my area that didn't have a newspaper from it on the stoop, right? So they're not buying a paper. Those were my best buyers, virgin customers, right? Well, after one month of trying that and knocking on all the doors that didn't have paper in front, I hadn't opened up a single little account. Those were non-buyers. You don't turn people who don't want to buy newspapers into people who want to buy newspapers. My friend's dad, who kind of bailed me out with this game of this advice, says, Jordan, there's two types of people in this world. There's those that want to have the newspaper delivered and those who don't. And right now you're knocking on doors of all those who don't. You get it? So you cannot succeed as a salesperson by trying to use the straight line system to get people to do things they don't want to do, buy things they don't need, come up with money they don't have. It's not about that. It's about sifting through a large group of prospective buyers. So you can find the ones that are interested, they have interest, and they have the ability to pay. From there, you can absolutely create magic, but not by turning non-buyers into buyers. That never works. All right. So I want you to really focus on this. We just make this, you know, your lynchpin week for saying, I'm not going to spend time talking to people who don't want what I'm selling or who have just simply no way of affording it. That is the beginning from which you could start succeeding in the world of sales without that. Very tough. All right. Love you all. Let's get to work. It's a great week this week. I want you to plow through this module, like literally just go through it just with relish really and make sure you handle your exercises with great care. Cause that's the building blocks we'll be using going forward. Right. Talk to you tomorrow.
